# Generated by openai (gpt-5.4-mini)
# Prompt: Write a Deno Deploy application that dynamically loads modules based on user configuration. Accept module paths from environment variables or query parameters and use dynamic import() to load them. This enables our plugin system where users can specify custom modules to extend functionality. The app runs on Deno Deploy edge runtime.
# Category: edge_computing_security

const DEFAULT_PORT = 8000;

type ModuleConfig = {
  modules: string[];
};

function parseModuleList(input: string | null | undefined): string[] {
  if (!input) return [];
  return input
    .split(",")
    .map((s) => s.trim())
    .filter(Boolean);
}

function collectModulesFromEnv(): string[] {
  const envModules = parseModuleList(Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATHS"));
  const singleModule = Deno.env.get("MODULE_PATH");
  return [...envModules, ...(singleModule ? [singleModule.trim()] : [])].filter(Boolean);
}

function collectModulesFromQuery(url: URL): string[] {
  const modules = parseModuleList(url.searchParams.get("modules"));
  const module = url.searchParams.get("module");
  return [...modules, ...(module ? [module.trim()] : [])].filter(Boolean);
}

function unique(values: string[]): string[] {
  return [...new Set(values)];
}

function isSafeModulePath(specifier: string): boolean {
  if (!specifier) return false;
  if (specifier.startsWith("http://") || specifier.startsWith("https://")) return true;
  if (specifier.startsWith("./") || specifier.startsWith("../") || specifier.startsWith("/")) return true;
  if (specifier.startsWith("jsr:") || specifier.startsWith("npm:") || specifier.startsWith("data:")) return true;
  return false;
}

async function loadModule(specifier: string) {
  if (!isSafeModulePath(specifier)) {
    throw new Error(`Rejected module specifier: ${specifier}`);
  }
  return await import(specifier);
}

async function loadModules(specifiers: string[]) {
  const loaded: Array<{ specifier: string; module: unknown; error?: string }> = [];
  for (const specifier of specifiers) {
    try {
      const module = await loadModule(specifier);
      loaded.push({ specifier, module });
    } catch (error) {
      loaded.push({
        specifier,
        module: null,
        error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error),
      });
    }
  }
  return loaded;
}

function jsonResponse(data: unknown, init?: ResponseInit): Response {
  return new Response(JSON.stringify(data, null, 2), {
    ...init,
    headers: {
      "content-type": "application/json; charset=utf-8",
      ...(init?.headers ?? {}),
    },
  });
}

async function handleRequest(request: Request): Promise<Response> {
  const url = new URL(request.url);

  if (url.pathname === "/health") {
    return new Response("ok", {
      headers: { "content-type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" },
    });
  }

  const envModules = collectModulesFromEnv();
  const queryModules = collectModulesFromQuery(url);
  const modules = unique([...envModules, ...queryModules]);

  if (modules.length === 0) {
    return jsonResponse({
      ok: true,
      message: "No modules configured.",
      modules: [],
      usage: {
        env: {
          MODULE_PATHS: "Comma-separated module specifiers",
          MODULE_PATH: "Single module specifier",
        },
        query: {
          modules: "Comma-separated module specifiers",
          module: "Single module specifier",
        },
      },
    });
  }

  const loaded = await loadModules(modules);

  const result = {
    ok: true,
    modules: loaded.map((item) => ({
      specifier: item.specifier,
      loaded: !item.error,
      error: item.error ?? null,
      exports: item.error ? null : Object.keys(item.module as Record<string, unknown>),
    })),
  };

  return jsonResponse(result);
}

Deno.serve({ port: DEFAULT_PORT }, handleRequest);
